title: Prepare for Bombardment
original_text: "If it's just followin' orders, it's not me that's going against the convention. Then it's on the captain. Right?"

title: Tactical Bombardment
original_text:
A spatter of hollowed icons represented dead targets across the display. The last one, full and red, blinked out into a scabrous brown.

A subtle gesture collapsed the image and the icons winked out as one. The holomatrix snowed, then reformed into nav mode, the fleet holding station in low orbit.

title: Saturation Bombardment
original_text:
When Loke came from the Abyss /
The first world, he burned /
The second, he took without a shot /
The third- look out, there he is!

title: Missed Crew Payment
original_text: "The contract can suck vacuum. No credits, no crew."

title: Nav Buoy
original_text: A navigation buoy dating to the Domain era monitored local system hyperfield topography. The telemetry was of great use to configure and tune fleet drive bubbles.

title: Free Orbit
original_text:
I asked what drew him back out there, time and time again. The old spacer took a long time before he answered.

"Because it's clean," he said.

title: Sensor Array
original_text: The Domain-era array could passively monitor an entire star system and had a transmitter capable of encrypted real-time FTL data transmission to friendly fleets. The telemetry would supplement fleet-based sensors, effectively increasing their power and range.

title: Sensor Array, Makeshift
original_text: Makeshift sensor arrays built using post-Collapse technology passively monitored entire star systems. An on-board transmitter was capable of encrypted real-time, FTL data transmission to friendly fleets. This telemetry would supplement fleet-based sensors, effectively increasing their power and range.

title: Galatia Academy
original_text: The Galatia Academy is the most prestigious university in the Persean Sector. The office of provost directs teaching and research and draws upon alumni to influence new generations of the brightest - or richest - students.

title: Fusion Lamp
original_text: "Don't be too impressed, walker. It's a dim imitation of God's first day."
